findings.title: Command injection via unsanitized path concatenated into execSync shell command
findings.evidence: execSync("tar -czf out.tgz " + path) concatenates the untrusted `path` argument directly into a shell command string. A value like `; rm -rf /` or `$(...)` would be executed by the shell, yielding arbitrary command execution. The new import `import { execSync } from "node:child_process";` confirms a subprocess is spawned with attacker-controllable input.

notes: Diff Drift correctly flagged the child_process import at high severity; the concrete risk is the string concatenation of `path` into the execSync command, not merely the import itself.
Only one file (src/archive.ts) changed; nothing was skipped as too large, and the raw diff fully corroborates the report.
Fix would be to use execFileSync('tar', ['-czf','out.tgz', path]) to avoid shell interpretation, or validate/escape the path.

evaluator.note: Benchmark v4 blind run against the engine-v2 binary; packet-only context.
